SEARCH
Package client.view

Class WaitlistController

java.lang.Object
client.view.BaseController
client.view.WaitlistController
public class WaitlistController extends BaseController
"My Waiting List" screen (visitor-facing): the logged-in visitor's WAITING reservations, fetched via LIST_WAITLIST. Each row carries the queued reservation plus the entry's grab-offer columns, so a row with a live offer is shown prominently with a one-second countdown to grab_expires_at and an Accept button (ACCEPT_GRAB); every row has a Leave/Decline button (LEAVE_WAITLIST). The server is the source of truth — these buttons just fire the ops; the price was already set server-side at join and is never recomputed here.

Realtime. A grab offer does not change the reservation row (it stays WAITING) — its only push is the notification the shell already pops up. To make the offer/Accept appear live, this screen attaches a local-only EventBus callback to the actor's ("notification", actorId) key and reloads on any notification. It must not send its own SUBSCRIBE/UNSUBSCRIBE for that key: the shell owns the per-connection server subscription, and a per-screen UNSUBSCRIBE on hide would tear down the shell's popup feed. It additionally subscribes (via BaseController.subscribe(java.lang.String, long, java.util.function.Consumer<common.dto.ServerEvent>)) to each WAITING reservation id so an accept/leave/cancel committed elsewhere also refreshes the list.

    WaitlistController.TickTarget
    One offered row's countdown state: where to write, and which Accept to disable at zero.

    • WaitlistController

      public WaitlistController(NetworkService network, Session session)
      Creates the waiting-list controller.
      Parameters:
      network - the shared network service
      session - the current client session

  • Method Details

    • initialize

      private void initialize()
      FXML lifecycle hook: starts the offer feed, countdown, and initial load.

    • onRefresh

      private void onRefresh()
      Refresh-button handler: reloads the waiting list.

    • loadParks

      private void loadParks()
      Loads park id → name once, then loads the waiting list.

    • load

      private void load()
      Loads the visitor's WAITING entries and repaints.

    • populate

      private void populate(List<WaitlistEntryDTO> rows)
      Renders the header and one row per waiting entry (offer rows stand out).
      Parameters:
      rows - the waiting-list entries to display

    • resubscribe

      private void resubscribe(List<WaitlistEntryDTO> rows)
      Points this screen's per-reservation subscriptions at exactly the WAITING reservation ids on screen, dropping the previous set first (also sends the matching UNSUBSCRIBE). The notification piggyback is separate and untouched.
      Parameters:
      rows - the currently displayed waiting entries to subscribe to

    • onReservationEvent

      private void onReservationEvent(ServerEvent ev)
      Reacts to a pushed reservation change by reloading the list.
      Parameters:
      ev - the pushed event

    • subscribeToOffers

      private void subscribeToOffers()
      Attaches a local-only callback to the actor's notification key so a freshly arrived grab offer (whose only push is the notification) refreshes this list live. Deliberately sends no wire SUBSCRIBE — the shell owns that.

    • onHideHook

      protected void onHideHook()
      Description copied from class: BaseController
      Overridable template for additional cleanup beyond unsubscribing. Default is a no-op.
      Overrides:
      onHideHook in class BaseController

    • startCountdown

      private void startCountdown()
      Starts the one-second timeline that updates offer countdowns.

    • tick

      private void tick()
      Recomputes each offered row's time-left, flipping to "expired" at zero.

    • formatRemaining

      private static String formatRemaining(long secs)
      Formats a seconds count as mm:ss.
      Parameters:
      secs - the remaining seconds
      Returns:
      the mm:ss string

    • accept

      private void accept(int reservationId)
      Accepts a grab offer, then reloads on success.
      Parameters:
      reservationId - the offered WAITING reservation to claim

    • leave

      private void leave(int reservationId)
      Leaves the waiting list (or declines an offer) after a confirmation prompt.
      Parameters:
      reservationId - the WAITING reservation to remove from the queue

    • confirmAction

      private boolean confirmAction(String header, String content)
      Shows a blocking Yes/No confirmation and returns true only if the user clicked Yes. Both the Leave and the offer-row Decline buttons fire LEAVE_WAITLIST through leave(int), so guarding it here means a stray click never drops the visitor from the queue without their agreeing first. Mirrors ReservationListController's Confirm/Cancel prompt.
      Parameters:
      header - the bold dialog header (the question)
      content - the explanatory body line
      Returns:
      whether the user confirmed

    • headerRow

      private HBox headerRow()
      Returns the table header row of column titles.
      Returns:
      the table header row of column titles

    • flexSpacer

      private Region flexSpacer()
      A zero-width filler that absorbs the row's slack so fixed columns and the action buttons keep their natural widths instead of being squeezed.
      Returns:
      a horizontally-growing spacer region

    • dataRow

      private HBox dataRow(WaitlistEntryDTO e, boolean withDivider)
      Builds one waiting-list row: an offer row (countdown + Accept/Decline) or a plain WAITING row (status + Leave).
      Parameters:
      e - the waiting entry to render
      withDivider - whether to draw a divider below the row
      Returns:
      the assembled row

    • isOffered

      private boolean isOffered(WaitlistEntryDTO e)
      True when the entry currently holds a parseable grab offer (offered + has an expiry).
      Parameters:
      e - the waiting entry
      Returns:
      whether the entry has a live, parseable grab offer

      Returns:
